Subtitle: Explaining GNN with High-Level Concepts — Master’s Thesis
Description: Designed and implemented a Python-based data processing pipeline to convert raw inputs into heterogeneous graph formats using PyTorch Geometric.
Leveraged large language model (LLM) pipelines to extract high-level groupings and concepts, integrating them into a domain ontology for richer semantic modeling.
Developed and trained multiple Graph Neural Network (GNN) architectures on both homogeneous and heterogeneous graph data.
Employed the EvoLearner class from the Ontolearn library to generate explanations for GNN predictions and evaluate the impact of concept integration on model behavior.

Subtitle: Psychological State Diagnosis using Deep Learning — Bachelor’s Major Project
Description: Designed and implemented a multimodal depression-detection system in Python combining EEG signals from wearable devices, facial emotion recognition via smartphone camera, and PHQ-9 questionnaire data.
Processed brain-wave signals (α, β, γ, θ, δ), facial emotion frames, and demographic inputs to train deep-learning models for psychological state classification.
Built an end-to-end pipeline for data acquisition, preprocessing, CNN-based emotion recognition, and neural network training.
Enabled objective mental-health screening using involuntary physiological and behavioral cues in addition to self-reported data.
